subject: The EdTech brands winning on acquisition spend less on ads, not more
body:
Hi [Name],

Here's something counterintuitive: the fastest-growing online education brands in 2024 cut paid acquisition budgets by 20% and grew enrollment by 35%. How?

They shifted spend toward alumni activation — turning graduates into a referral engine. Turns out a peer recommendation converts 5x better than a retargeting ad.

[Product] builds and automates your alumni community flywheel — from post-completion nurture to structured referral incentives.

One client generated 40% of new enrollments from alumni within 6 months of launch.

Want to see the playbook?
why_it_works: data-driven with contrarian hook + mechanism explanation + social proof + offer
